title: CSW Industrials: Contractor Loyalty, Compounding at a Discount
author: Gurufocus
description: CSW Industrials has built a decade-long compounding story through disciplined acquisitions in contractor-focused channels (HVAC, plumbing, electrical). Recent large acquisitions of Aspen Manufacturing ($313.5M) and MARS Parts ($650M) have created near-term earnings dilution and integration overhead, but management targets normalized adjusted EBITDA exceeding $300M annually post-synergy. The stock has pulled back 22% from highs to ~$263, trading at ~17x forward EBITDA. The investment case hinges on successful cross-selling, margin expansion, and deleveraging over the next 2-3 years, with organic volume recovery dependent on HVAC destocking cycles completing.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite near-term earnings headwinds from integration costs and soft organic volumes, the company demonstrates a proven decade-long acquisition playbook with strong historical returns (19% incremental FCF yield on deployed capital). The current pullback to 17x forward EBITDA offers attractive entry for patient capital. Management's track record, strong contractor-channel moat with high switching costs, and clear path to normalized $300M+ EBITDA support a positive long-term outlook, though near-term execution risks exist around MARS/Aspen integration and organic volume recovery.

title: CSW Industrials Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.27 Per Share
author: Globe Newswire
description: CSW Industrials announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.27 per share, payable on February 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 30, 2026. The diversified industrial company operates in three segments: Contractor Solutions, Specialized Reliability Solutions, and Engineered Building Solutions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a regular quarterly dividend, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Consistent dividend payments are typically viewed as a positive indicator of company health and confidence in future cash flows.

title: CSW Industrials Deploys Over $26.5 Million on Acquisitions Within the Specialized Reliability Solutions Segment, Diversifying Our End Markets
author: Globe Newswire
description: CSW Industrials acquired Hydrotex Holdings and ProAction Fluids for over $26.5 million, expanding its Specialized Reliability Solutions segment and diversifying end markets in specialty lubricants and infrastructure drilling products.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is strategically expanding its portfolio, investing in new markets, and expecting earnings accretion from the acquisitions

title: CSW Industrials Completes Previously Announced Accretive, Complementary, and Synergistic Acquisition of Motors & Armatures Parts
author: Joseph B. Armes
description: CSW Industrials acquired Motors & Armatures Parts for $650 million, expanding its HVAC/R product portfolio and adding approximately 200 new employees. The acquisition is expected to drive above-market growth and diversify repair and replacement solutions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Completed a strategic acquisition that expands product portfolio, adds new employees, and is expected to drive growth in a profitable market segment

title: CSW Industrials Announces Definitive Agreement to Acquire Motors & Armatures Parts for $650 Million, Accelerating Disciplined Capital Allocation and Capitalizing on Complementary Portfolio in the HVAC/R End Market
author: Globe Newswire
description: CSW Industrials will acquire Motors & Armatures Parts for $650 million in cash, expanding its HVAC/R product portfolio and expected to be immediately accretive to earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is strategically expanding its product portfolio, expects immediate earnings accretion, and anticipates significant cost synergies from the acquisition
